zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford於1286年出生在英國英格蘭素瑞。她和Richard de Ford至少有1個女兒。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford于1286年出生在英国英格兰素瑞。她和Richard de Ford至少有1个女儿。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived near a ford (Middle English, Old English ford), or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, such as Ford (Durham, Herefordshire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Sussex), Ford in Sefton (Lancashire), Ford in Crediton and Ford in Holcombe Rogus (both Devon), Ford in Litton and Ford in Wiveliscombe (both Somerset).

Irish: Anglicized form (quasi-translation) of various Gaelic names, for example MacGiolla na Naomh ‘son of Gilla na Naomh’ (a personal name meaning ‘servant of the saints’), Mac Conshámha ‘son of Conshnámha’ (a personal name composed of the elements con ‘dog’ + snámh ‘to swim’), in all of which the final syllable was wrongly thought to be áth ‘ford’, and Ó Fuar(th)áin (see Foran ).

variants.name: Forth
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived by a ford, Middle High German vort, vurt, Middle Low German vorde ‘ford, entrance way’, or a habitational name from a place in Franconia or in former Pomerania (now Poland) named Forth.

variants.name: Forde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Declan, Dermot, Aine, Fionnuala, Liam, Nuala.
English and Irish: variant of   Ford   1 and 2. This is a very common spelling in Ireland.
Norwegian (Førde): habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Førde (there are eleven on the west coast), from Old Norse fyrthi, dative of fjǫrthr ‘fjord’.

variants.name: Foran
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fuartháin ‘descendant of Fuarthán’, a personal name derived from fuar ‘cold’. The word fuarán also means ‘spring’ or ‘pool’. The form Fuarthán was understood as fuarathán ‘cold little ford’, and the name has thus sometimes been translated into English as Ford .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford was born in 1286, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter with Richard de Ford. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford est née en 1286, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Richard de Ford. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford wurde 1286 in Surrey, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Richard de Ford mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford è nata il nel 1286 nel Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Richard de Ford. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Surrey, England, United Kingdom: イギリスイギリススリー
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Fordは1286年にイギリスイギリススリーで生まれた。彼女はRichard de Fordとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford(은)는 1286년 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Richard de Ford(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford nasceu em 1286, em Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Richard de Ford. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford родилась в 1286 г. в местности сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Richard de Ford. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Ford nació en 1286, en Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Richard de Ford. Murió en su pueblo.
